question.text: An option where the spot price is higher than the strike price is classified as:

answer.text: In-the-money (ITM) for a call option and Out-of-the-money (OTM) for a put option
In-the-money for both call and put options
Out-of-the-money for a call option and In-the-money for a put option
At-the-money for both call and put options

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When spot > strike: Call is ITM (buyer profits by exercising the right to buy below market). Put is OTM (no benefit to exercise the right to sell below market). **The Trap:** The moneyness reverses for puts — a high spot price makes puts worthless. ITM/OTM classification depends on the option type. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII and I cover moneyness classification identically. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** ITM options have intrinsic value; OTM options have only time value. As expiry approaches, only ITM options retain value — OTM options decay to zero.
